config.stageStaleWarning: This stage is no longer available.
config.triggerOnFailure: Trigger even if this pipeline fails
config.webhookStatusNotConfiguredDescription: The webhook is not yet set up. Click the button to configure it manually.
config.webhookBranchFilterDescription: Only trigger builds for matching branches. Supports comma-separated values and wildcards (e.g. feature/*). Leave empty to accept all branches.
config.cloneCommitHashDescription: Leave empty to use the latest commit of the branch
config.cloneSubmodulesDescription: Recursively clone all git submodules declared in .gitmodules
config.webhookUrlTooltipDesc: A POST request with the following JSON body will be sent to this URL:
config.triggerOnSuccessDesc: only if the pipeline completed without errors
config.triggerOnFailureDesc: only if at least one node failed
config.cleanWorkdirInfo: Removes the cloned repository directory from disk after the pipeline completes.
config.envVarsInfo: Injects the repository environment variables into the pipeline, making them available to subsequent nodes. Variables accumulate throughout the pipeline.
config.saveVersionInfo: Saves the current build as a new numbered version using the built image tag and commit info.
config.downloadFilenamePlaceholder: Leave empty to use filename from URL
config.logSinceTooltip: Filter logs starting from this point in time. Accepts a relative duration (e.g. 30s, 5m, 2h) or an ISO timestamp (e.g. 2024-01-15T10:30:00). Useful to avoid matching old logs that predate the current deployment.
config.sonarqubeOrganizationDescription: Required for SonarCloud. Leave empty for self-hosted SonarQube.
config.sonarqubeVersionDescription: Docker image tag to use, e.g. community, lts-community, latest.
config.sonarqubeProjectKeyTooltip: A unique identifier for your project in SonarQube (e.g. my-org_my-app). It must be unique on the server and may only contain letters, digits, '-', '_', '.' and ':'. Leave empty to auto-generate one from the repository name.
config.sonarqubeTokenDescription: SonarQube user or project token. Leave empty to use default admin credentials for local mode.
config.sonarqubeExclusionsDescription: Comma-separated glob patterns to exclude from analysis.
config.sonarqubeQualityGateDescription: Fail the pipeline if the quality gate does not pass.
config.sonarqubeEnforceMinScoreDescription: Fail the pipeline if the selected metric is below the required minimum (e.g. coverage must exceed 80).
config.sonarqubeMinScoreDescription: The analysed metric must be greater than or equal to this value (0-100).
config.releaseNotesPlaceholder: Describe the changes in this release...
config.certificateDescription: Select a certificate configured in the SSL Certificates section
config.certificateRequired: An SSL certificate is required when HTTPS is enabled
config.sslDomainMustPoint: The domain must point to this server's IP address before issuing

pipeline.dragHint: Drag & drop into any compatible inputs to use as value
pipeline.staleRef: Source node removed or disabled — click × to clear

inputs.desc_envVariables: Repository environment variables injected into the pipeline
inputs.desc_workDir: Path to the cloned repository on disk
inputs.desc_branch: Git branch that was checked out
inputs.desc_commitHash: Full SHA hash of the checked-out commit
inputs.desc_commitMessage: Commit message of the checked-out commit
inputs.desc_imageName: Full Docker image name including tag
inputs.desc_imageId: Docker image ID assigned after build
inputs.desc_remote: Remote URL the tag was pushed to
inputs.desc_environmentId: ID of the environment that was set
inputs.desc_registryUrl: Registry URL the image was pushed to
inputs.desc_fileName: Name of the backup archive uploaded to S3
inputs.desc_sizeBytes: Size of the file or cache in bytes
inputs.desc_found: Whether the pattern was found in logs
inputs.desc_matchedLine: First log line matching the pattern
inputs.desc_passed: Whether the condition evaluated to true
inputs.desc_delayed: Number of seconds the pipeline was delayed
inputs.desc_outputFile: Full path to the downloaded file
inputs.desc_targetDir: Directory where the repository was cloned
inputs.desc_serviceName: Docker service or Swarm service name
inputs.desc_image: Image name that was scanned or tagged
inputs.desc_sent: Whether the notification was sent successfully (HTTP 200)
inputs.desc_composePath: Resolved path to the Compose file
inputs.desc_sourceImage: Source image name used for tagging
inputs.desc_targetTag: New tag applied to the image
inputs.desc_qualityGatePassed: Whether the SonarQube quality gate passed
inputs.desc_projectKey: SonarQube project key that was analysed
inputs.desc_score: Value of the analysed metric (e.g. coverage) when a minimum score is enforced
inputs.desc_releaseId: ID of the release created on GitHub or GitLab
inputs.desc_releaseUrl: URL of the published release page
inputs.desc_description: Short description shown next to the commit status
inputs.desc_domainId: Traefik router identifier for the domain
inputs.desc_containerPort: Port exposed by the container for this domain
inputs.desc_domainRemoved: Whether the domain was successfully removed

pipeline.noInputsAvailable: No inputs available from previous nodes
pipeline.nodeOutputsHint: Values this node will produce for downstream nodes

stages.manageDescription: Configure the deployment stages (pre-prod, prod, …) for this repository.
stages.infoDescription: Stages let you deploy the same repository to several isolated environments (for example pre-production and production) from a single codebase. Each stage runs its own pipeline, Traefik routing, and keeps its own scoped environment variables. This lets you validate a build on a pre-prod stage before promoting it to production, without ever mixing configuration or secrets between environments.
stages.stagesCount: {count, plural, =0 {No stages} one {# stage} other {# stages}}
stages.noStages: No stages for this repository yet
stages.addNewStageDescription: Create a new deployment stage for this repository.
stages.removeDescription: This will remove the stage and its scoped variables. This action cannot be undone.
stages.cannotDeleteProduction: The production stage cannot be deleted
stages.requiredStageHint: When set, this stage can no longer be built directly: only the selected stage can build it through its "Trigger Stage Build" node.
stages.protectedTooltip: This stage cannot be built directly — only « {stage} » can build it via its "Trigger Stage Build" node
